label: OpenUI Public Skills Starter
summary: Official repo-owned skill product line for plugin-grade MCP distribution. This surface packages current contract templates, sample host configs, proof loops, and install/use guidance without claiming a marketplace listing or a managed runtime.

officialPublicSurfaces.codex: Official MCP install docs exist; this starter manifest only claims the repo-owned public package shelf.
officialPublicSurfaces.claudeCode: Official MCP install docs exist; this starter manifest only claims the repo-owned public package shelf.
officialPublicSurfaces.openclaw: ClawHub exists as the official public surface, but this manifest does not claim a live OpenUI listing there.

audience: builders who need a bounded official skill product line before any future Skills runtime exists
operators who want plugin-grade starter bundles for Codex, Claude Code, or OpenClaw-style hosts
role: Official repo-owned skill product line for honest host installs and unlisted packaging.
installPath: Copy the host config that matches your client from examples/public-distribution/*.json
Use examples/skills/starter-contract.template.json only when you need to draft a repo-specific skill contract
Use examples/public-distribution/openclaw-public-ready.manifest.json when you need the repo-owned OpenClaw public-ready bundle
usePath: Start from the current local stdio MCP builder surface
Only mention the OpenAPI contract as a compatibility bridge
Only mention repo-local workflow packets as maintainer-facing read-only surfaces
Use proof anchors from repo-owned files instead of future-product promises
Pair the starter bundle with openui-mcp-studio ecosystem-guide --json and npm run repo:doctor when you need the shortest proof loop

proofAnchors.why: States the current builder-surface order and keeps Skills honest.
Repeats the builder-order boundary and non-goals.
Defines the compatibility-bridge boundary without turning it into a hosted API claim.
Exposes repo-local builder commands that this skill product line points readers toward.
Provides the repo-owned OpenClaw public-ready bundle without claiming an official catalog.

notFor: claiming a shipped formal Skills runtime
claiming marketplace or plugin listing support
claiming SDK or hosted API maturity
reframing OpenUI MCP Studio as a generic external integration platform
claiming an official OpenClaw runtime or ClawHub listing
operatorOnlyFollowThrough: If this skill product line is later promoted into a public runtime, shared docs, contracts, CLI, and verification must all be updated in the same wave.
Any future marketplace submission or vendor review remains outside this starter pack.
